mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS) above 100k signals weakening labor market conditions, increasing recession risks and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent rise in housing starts (HOUST) and real retail sales (RSAFS) indicates strengthening consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between HOUST and RSAFS shows negative coefficient in 3-year rolling OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100 signals weakening manufacturing activity, increasing recession risks and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation reading above 3.5% triggers central bank hawkishness, leading to higher real rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction (GDPC1 < 0) signals economic recession, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in unemployment (UNRATE > 4.5%) and decline in payroll employment (PAYEMS < 0) signals labor market deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term between UNRATE and PAYEMS shows positive coefficient in 3-year rolling OLS regression.

mechanism_narrative: A 20-day lagged correlation between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL above 0.6 indicates policy rate misalignment with inflation, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction term falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.
